13:     # Merges in the conditions from <tt>other</tt>, if <tt>other</tt> is an ActiveRecord::Relation.
14:     # Returns an array representing the intersection of the resulting records with <tt>other</tt>, if <tt>other</tt> is an array.
15:     #
16:     #   Post.where(published: true).joins(:comments).merge( Comment.where(spam: false) )
17:     #   # Performs a single join query with both where conditions.
18:     #
19:     #   recent_posts = Post.order('created_at DESC').first(5)
20:     #   Post.where(published: true).merge(recent_posts)
21:     #   # Returns the intersection of all published posts with the 5 most recently created posts.
22:     #   # (This is just an example. You'd probably want to do this with a single query!)
23:     #
24:     # Procs will be evaluated by merge:
25:     #
26:     #   Post.where(published: true).merge(-> { joins(:comments) })
27:     #   # => Post.where(published: true).joins(:comments)
28:     #
29:     # This is mainly intended for sharing common conditions between multiple associations.
30:     #
31:     # For conditions that exist in both relations, those from <tt>other</tt> will take precedence.
32:     # To find the intersection of two relations, use QueryMethods#and.
33:     def merge(other, *rest)
34:       if other.is_a?(Array)
35:         records & other
36:       elsif other
37:         spawn.merge!(other, *rest)
38:       else
39:         raise ArgumentError, "invalid argument: #{other.inspect}."
40:       end
41:     end

45:         Relation::HashMerger.new(self, other).merge
46:       elsif other.is_a?(Relation)
47:         Relation::Merger.new(self, other).merge
48:       elsif other.respond_to?(:to_proc)
49:         instance_exec(&other)
50:       else
51:         raise ArgumentError, "#{other.inspect} is not an ActiveRecord::Relation"
52:       end

60:         # If it returns +nil+ or +false+, an
61:         # {all}[rdoc-ref:Scoping::Named::ClassMethods#all] scope is returned instead.
62:         #
63:         # A \scope represents a narrowing of a database query, such as
64:         # <tt>where(color: :red).select('shirts.*').includes(:washing_instructions)</tt>.
65:         #

67:         #     scope :red, -> { where(color: 'red') }

69:         #   end

72:         # <tt>Shirt.dry_clean_only</tt>. <tt>Shirt.red</tt>, in effect,
73:         # represents the query <tt>Shirt.where(color: 'red')</tt>.

82:         #   end

84:         # Unlike <tt>Shirt.find(...)</tt>, however, the object returned by
85:         # <tt>Shirt.red</tt> is not an Array but an ActiveRecord::Relation,
86:         # which is composable with other scopes; it resembles the association object
87:         # constructed by a {has_many}[rdoc-ref:Associations::ClassMethods#has_many]
88:         # declaration. For instance, you can invoke <tt>Shirt.red.first</tt>, <tt>Shirt.red.count</tt>,
89:         # <tt>Shirt.red.where(size: 'small')</tt>. Also, just as with the
90:         # association objects, named \scopes act like an Array, implementing
91:         # Enumerable; <tt>Shirt.red.each(&block)</tt>, <tt>Shirt.red.first</tt>,
92:         # and <tt>Shirt.red.inject(memo, &block)</tt> all behave as if
93:         # <tt>Shirt.red</tt> really was an array.
94:         #
95:         # These named \scopes are composable. For instance,
96:         # <tt>Shirt.red.dry_clean_only</tt> will produce all shirts that are
97:         # both red and dry clean only. Nested finds and calculations also work
98:         # with these compositions: <tt>Shirt.red.dry_clean_only.count</tt>
99:         # returns the number of garments for which these criteria obtain.
100:         # Similarly with <tt>Shirt.red.dry_clean_only.average(:thread_count)</tt>.
101:         #
102:         # All scopes are available as class methods on the ActiveRecord::Base
103:         # descendant upon which the \scopes were defined. But they are also
104:         # available to {has_many}[rdoc-ref:Associations::ClassMethods#has_many]

106:         #
107:         #   class Person < ActiveRecord::Base

109:         #   end
110:         #
111:         # then <tt>elton.shirts.red.dry_clean_only</tt> will return all of
112:         # Elton's red, dry clean only shirts.

114:         # \Named scopes can also have extensions, just as with
115:         # {has_many}[rdoc-ref:Associations::ClassMethods#has_many] declarations:
116:         #
117:         #   class Shirt < ActiveRecord::Base
118:         #     scope :red, -> { where(color: 'red') } do
119:         #       def dom_id
120:         #         'red_shirts'
121:         #       end
122:         #     end
123:         #   end
124:         #
125:         # Scopes can also be used while creating/building a record.
126:         #
127:         #   class Article < ActiveRecord::Base
128:         #     scope :published, -> { where(published: true) }
129:         #   end
130:         #
131:         #   Article.published.new.published    # => true
132:         #   Article.published.create.published # => true
133:         #
134:         # \Class methods on your model are automatically available
135:         # on scopes. Assuming the following setup:
136:         #
137:         #   class Article < ActiveRecord::Base
138:         #     scope :published, -> { where(published: true) }
139:         #     scope :featured, -> { where(featured: true) }
140:         #
141:         #     def self.latest_article
142:         #       order('published_at desc').first
143:         #     end
144:         #
145:         #     def self.titles

147:         #     end
148:         #   end
149:         #
150:         # We are able to call the methods like this:
151:         #
152:         #   Article.published.featured.latest_article
153:         #   Article.featured.titles

5:     # = Active Record Collection Proxy
6:     #
7:     # Collection proxies in Active Record are middlemen between an
8:     # <tt>association</tt>, and its <tt>target</tt> result set.
9:     #
10:     # For example, given
11:     #
12:     #   class Blog < ActiveRecord::Base
13:     #     has_many :posts
14:     #   end
15:     #
16:     #   blog = Blog.first
17:     #
18:     # The collection proxy returned by <tt>blog.posts</tt> is built from a
19:     # <tt>:has_many</tt> <tt>association</tt>, and delegates to a collection
20:     # of posts as the <tt>target</tt>.
21:     #
22:     # This class delegates unknown methods to the <tt>association</tt>'s
23:     # relation class via a delegate cache.
24:     #
25:     # The <tt>target</tt> result set is not loaded until needed. For example,
26:     #
27:     #   blog.posts.count
28:     #
29:     # is computed directly through SQL and does not trigger by itself the
30:     # instantiation of the actual post records.
31:     class CollectionProxy < Relation
32:       def initialize(klass, association, **) # :nodoc:
33:         @association = association
34:         super klass
35: 
36:         extensions = association.extensions
37:         extend(*extensions) if extensions.any?
38:       end

41:         @association.target
42:       end
43:

45:         @association.load_target
46:       end

50:       #   person.pets.loaded? # => false
51:       #   person.pets.records
52:       #   person.pets.loaded? # => true
53:       def loaded?
54:         @association.loaded?
55:       end

61:       # :call-seq:
62:       #   select(*fields, &block)
63:       #
64:       # Works in two ways.
65:       #
66:       # *First:* Specify a subset of fields to be selected from the result set.
67:       #
68:       #   class Person < ActiveRecord::Base
69:       #     has_many :pets
70:       #   end
71:       #
72:       #   person.pets
73:       #   # => [
74:       #   #       #<Pet id: 1, name: "Fancy-Fancy", person_id: 1>,
75:       #   #       #<Pet id: 2, name: "Spook", person_id: 1>,
76:       #   #       #<Pet id: 3, name: "Choo-Choo", person_id: 1>
77:       #   #    ]
78:       #
79:       #   person.pets.select(:name)
80:       #   # => [
81:       #   #      #<Pet id: nil, name: "Fancy-Fancy">,
82:       #   #      #<Pet id: nil, name: "Spook">,
83:       #   #      #<Pet id: nil, name: "Choo-Choo">
84:       #   #    ]
85:       #
86:       #   person.pets.select(:id, :name)
87:       #   # => [
88:       #   #      #<Pet id: 1, name: "Fancy-Fancy">,
89:       #   #      #<Pet id: 2, name: "Spook">,
90:       #   #      #<Pet id: 3, name: "Choo-Choo">
91:       #   #    ]
92:       #
93:       # Be careful because this also means you're initializing a model
94:       # object with only the fields that you've selected. If you attempt
95:       # to access a field except +id+ that is not in the initialized record you'll
96:       # receive:
97:       #
98:       #   person.pets.select(:name).first.person_id
99:       #   # => ActiveModel::MissingAttributeError: missing attribute 'person_id' for Pet
100:       #
101:       # *Second:* You can pass a block so it can be used just like <tt>Array#select</tt>.

105:       #

content.text:
## Step 1 — Orient in the codebase

This is the Rails monorepo. Active Record lives in `activerecord/`. The lazy query object returned by every finder/scope/association is `ActiveRecord::Relation`, defined in `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb`. It is not a single file: the query interface is split into modules under `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/`, value objects (`WhereClause`, `FromClause`, `QueryAttribute`), a `Merger`/`HashMerger`, and a dynamic delegation cache in `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/delegation.rb`. Association collections reuse the same contract via subclasses (`AssociationRelation`, `CollectionProxy`, `DisableJoinsAssociationRelation`).

Quick map for targeted lookups:

- Relation class & lifecycle: `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb`
- Query-building methods: `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb`
- Finders: `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/finder_methods.rb`

## Step 2 — Relation contract and shared behavior

`ActiveRecord::Relation` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:5`) exposes the query interface and owns the `@values` hash, `@table`, `@model`, `@loaded` flag, `@predicate_builder`, etc. Its public API comes almost entirely from the modules it includes:

- `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:69` — includes `FinderMethods`, `Calculations`, `SpawnMethods`, `QueryMethods`, `Batches`, `Explain`, `Delegation`
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:70` — includes `SignedId::RelationMethods`, `TokenFor::RelationMethods`

Module definitions:

- `FinderMethods` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/finder_methods.rb:6`
- `Calculations` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/calculations.rb:7`
- `SpawnMethods` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/spawn_methods.rb:8`
- `QueryMethods` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb:9`
- `Batches` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/batches.rb:7`
- `Explain` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/explain.rb:6`
- `Delegation` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/delegation.rb:5`

Supporting value/builder objects that make up the contract:

- `Relation::WhereClause` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/where_clause.rb:7`
- `Relation::FromClause` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/from_clause.rb:5`
- `Relation::QueryAttribute` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_attribute.rb:7`
- `Relation::Merger` / `Relation::HashMerger` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/merger.rb:43` and `:7`
- `PredicateBuilder` + handlers — `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/predicate_builder.rb`, including `RelationHandler` at `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/predicate_builder/relation_handler.rb:5`

What a teammate must know before changing the contract:

1. `Relation` stores all query state in `@values` and delegates to the model via `Delegation`. Changing how state is held (e.g. replacing `@values`) breaks `Merger`, `SpawnMethods`, every query method, and the dynamic delegate cache.
2. `SpawnMethods#spawn`/`initialize_copy` is the only sanctioned way to produce a new relation from an existing one; most query methods call `spawn` before mutating state.
3. `merge!` is the primitive for combining two relations; associations, default scopes, preloading, and named scopes all rely on it.
4. `arel`/`build_arel` turn the relation state into an Arel AST; `to_sql`, `exec_main_query`, and calculations all depend on this.
5. `Delegation` creates a per-model subclass of `Relation` and caches generated methods based on `Relation.public_instance_methods` — any change to the public method set invalidates or corrupts that cache.

## Step 3 — How a query is built up and executed

Order of layers, top to bottom:

1. **Model entry** — `ActiveRecord::Querying` delegates the whole query vocabulary to `:all` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/querying.rb:5`, `:24`).
2. **Scope factory** — `ActiveRecord::Scoping::Named#all` returns a relation, cloning the current scope or building a default-scoped one (`activerecord/lib/active_record/scoping/named.rb:22`).
3. **Relation construction** — `ActiveRecord::Core#relation` calls `Relation.create(self)` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/core.rb:442`); `Delegation::ClassMethods#create` picks the per-model delegate subclass (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/delegation.rb:139`).
4. **Per-model subclass creation** — `ActiveRecord::Base.extend Delegation::DelegateCache` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/base.rb:296`) creates a subclass for each model via `initialize_relation_delegate_cache` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/delegation.rb:31`).
5. **Chaining / mutation** — e.g. `where` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb:1103`) spawns a clone (`SpawnMethods#spawn`, `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/spawn_methods.rb:9`) and mutates `where_clause` via `where!` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb:1113`). `build_where_clause` builds the `WhereClause` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb:1692`).
6. **Merging** — `merge` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/spawn_methods.rb:33`) dispatches to `merge!` (`:43`), which uses `Relation::Merger` for relations (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/merger.rb:58`) or `HashMerger` for hashes (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/merger.rb:17`). Clause merge happens at `merger.rb:175-181`.
7. **SQL generation** — `to_sql` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:1243`) calls `arel` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb:1673`) → `build_arel` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb:1828`) → `connection.to_sql(arel)` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/connection_adapters/abstract/database_statements.rb:12`) → `to_sql_and_binds` (`:17`) compiles the Arel AST via the visitor.
8. **Execution** — iterating calls `records` → `load` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:353`, `1212`) → `exec_queries` (`1436`) → `exec_main_query` (`1460`) → `model._query_by_sql` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/querying.rb:67`) → `connection.select_all` (`activerecord/lib/active_record/connection_adapters/abstract/database_statements.rb:71`). `select_all` first unwraps a relation via `arel_from_relation` (`database_statements.rb:802`).

## Step 5 — How associations reuse the relation contract

Associations do not reimplement the query interface; they subclass or wrap `Relation`.

- `ActiveRecord::Associations::CollectionProxy < Relation` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/collection_proxy.rb:31`; initialized with an association (`:32`) and extends association extensions (`:37`).
- `ActiveRecord::AssociationRelation < Relation` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/association_relation.rb:4`; overrides `initialize`, `==`, bulk-insert guards, and `exec_queries` to set inverse instances (`:43`).
- `ActiveRecord::DisableJoinsAssociationRelation < Relation` — `activerecord/lib/active_record/disable_joins_association_relation.rb:4`; overrides `limit`, `first`, and `load` for has-many disable-joins behavior.

Where association queries are built:

- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/association.rb:315` — `target_scope` builds an `AssociationRelation` and merges `klass.scope_for_association`.
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/association.rb:113-115` — merges current global scope and association scope into `target_scope`.
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/association.rb:276` — merges `AssociationScope` constraints into the target scope.
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/association_scope.rb:21`, `:171` — creates the base relation via `reflection.build_scope` and evals scope blocks.
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/through_association.rb:37-38` — merges each reflection’s `scope_for_association`.
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/preloader/association.rb:301`, `:304` — merges reflection/preload scopes before loading.
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/disable_joins_association_scope.rb:37`, `:50` — merges scopes and creates a `DisableJoinsAssociationRelation`.
- `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/join_dependency.rb:189` — builds eager-load join constraints from the relation/alias tracker.

## Step 6 — Blast-radius assessment, ranked

High risk (will likely break if spawn/merge/execution/values contract changes):

1. `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/collection_proxy.rb:31` — subclasses `Relation` and overrides initialize; if `Relation#initialize` signature or spawn behavior changes, proxy construction breaks.
2. `activerecord/lib/active_record/association_relation.rb:4` — subclasses `Relation`, overrides `exec_queries`; any change to `exec_queries` args/contract breaks inverse-instance loading.
3. `activerecord/lib/active_record/disable_joins_association_relation.rb:4` — overrides `load`, `limit`, `first`; execution-path changes break ordered disable-joins collections.
4. `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/delegation.rb:31`, `:139` — per-model subclass creation and `ClassSpecificRelation#method_missing` rely on `Relation.public_instance_methods` and `spawn`; changing the public API or `initialize` invalidates the delegate cache.
5. `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/merger.rb:43` / `spawn_methods.rb:43` — `merge!` is the combining primitive used by scopes, associations, and default scopes; changing `values`, `where_clause`, or clause merge semantics breaks all merges.
6. `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/query_methods.rb:9` — every query method (`where`, `order`, `joins`, etc.) uses `spawn` and writes into `@values`/clause objects.
7. `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/finder_methods.rb:6` — `apply_join_dependency`/`exists?`/`last` depend on relation state and spawn.
8. `activerecord/lib/active_record/encryption/extended_deterministic_queries.rb:28` — prepends a module onto `ActiveRecord::Relation`; changing `scope_for_create` or `where_values_hash` signature breaks encrypted deterministic queries.
9. `activerecord/lib/active_record/associations/association.rb:113`, `:315` — association scope construction is all `merge!` calls; merge contract changes break associations.
10. `activerecord/lib/active_record/scoping/default.rb:145` — `build_default_scope` injects scopes into a base relation; relation-building changes break default scopes.

Medium risk:

11. `activerecord/lib/active_record/connection_handling.rb:419` — role/shard switching auto-loads returned relations; changes to `.load` or `is_a?` contract matter.
12. `activerecord/lib/active_record/sanitization.rb:213-214` — bind replacement case-matches `Relation` and calls `to_sql`; `to_sql` contract changes break sanitize.
13. `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:449`/`:454`/`:476`/`:483` — cache key/version use `to_sql`, `arel`, and internal values.
14. `activerecord/lib/active_record/relation/predicate_builder/relation_handler.rb:5` — relation-as-subquery depends on `select_values`, `model`, `table`, `eager_loading?`, `apply_join_dependency`.
15. `railties/lib/rails/commands/query/query_command.rb:172` — CLI renderer case-matches `ActiveRecord::Relation` and calls `offset`/`limit`/`to_sql`/`model`.
16. `activerecord/lib/active_record/signed_id.rb:32`, `token_for.rb:38` — finder mixins rely on `scoping` + `find_by`/`find`.

Low risk (mostly name/API-shape mirrors):

17. `activesupport/lib/active_support/core_ext/enumerable.rb:161` — `Enumerable#pick` mirrors `Relation#pick`; keep behavior in sync if `pick` semantics change.
18. `activesupport/lib/active_support/core_ext/hash/except.rb:8` — `Hash#except!` mirrors relation `except` naming only.
